description: A cooked sausage served in a soft split bun and commonly topped with mustard, ketchup, onions, relish, sauerkraut, or chili. Styles vary widely by city and region.
preparation_steps: Warm or lightly toast the bun.
Place the sausage in the bun.

nutrition.fat: Moderate to high depending on the sausage

health_advice.benefits: Convenient and filling, but not usually considered a nutrient-dense meal on its own.
health_advice.risks: Processed sausages are often high in sodium and saturated fat, especially with heavy toppings.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Pair with vegetables and choose simpler toppings and smaller portions.

dietary_notes.note: Classic hot dogs are non-vegetarian, but plant-based versions exist. Gluten-free buns are required for a gluten-free version.

allergens: Dairy if cheese topping is used
